pages.raw_text:
Howard to Bernard streets, were
adopted last night by the city coun-
Gil. Washington street was ordered
paved with granitoid or brick from
Second to Eighth avenues.


A number Of Spokane people, in
Guiding Sheriff Pugh, Jailer Max
Anderson, J. A. Yoemans of the Old
National bank and J. Lehman of
the postoffice have been called to
Colville as witnesses in the Logan
murder trial. Logan met the MUIR
dered woman, Mrs. Agnes Jansen,
in Spokane.


It is announced at the Sacred
Heart hospital that John S. De-
laney, the Great Northern detec-
tive, is improving. He was operated
on for appendicitis.


The o. 3. Nelson Co. Of Pateros,
Okanogan county, storekeepers, has
incorporated at the auditors office
for $4,000. O. B. Nelson and asso-
ciates of Spokane are the owners OF
the store.


w. n. Reyes, an inspecting en-
gineer of the reclamation work or
the interior department at Wash
ington, has arrived in Spokane and
opened headquarters at the federal
building. He will oversee the work
in four states.


r. M. Rothrock announces that
DR.. Melvin, head of the animal in
dustry bureau at Washington, has
removed the order requiring an in
spection of Washington sheep be-
fore leaving the state. This caused
some trouble and delay for sheep
being shipped out of the state. The
order was put in force because of
the existence of scab In some
localities.


The city council has rejected the
claim of S. T. Crane, the attorney,
for $4,000 damages for alleged false
arrest during the l. w. w. trouble.
The same fate met the claim of
Philip Manley for $4,000 for being
soused with water during the ear
lier stages of the fight.


Hereafter the city engineers Of
flee must make out detail state
ment of the engineering costs in
each street Improvement job that
passes through the office and file
the same with the report of the cost
of the job that goes to the city
council.
